_comment: [60.5] AIR FACILITIES -- the campaign's air map, transcribed from the chart the book prints. Source: docs/rules/60-scenario-group-one-the-italians.md lines 288-362, VERIFIED AGAINST THE SCAN: tmp/'The Campaign for North Africa.pdf' PDF page 79 (book folio 28), rendered at 600 dpi and read hex by hex with eyes on 2026-07-22, because a hex label is exactly the class of datum OCR corrupts and a wrong hex puts an airfield in the sea. Rule 64.3 makes Section 60 the full campaign's set-up, so this chart IS the campaign's air map. It replaces the VASSAL extraction's air_facility records (data/oob_italian.json), which carried 10 Air Landing Strips and 1 Alighting Area -- NO AIRFIELD AT ALL -- of which exactly one, Sollum C4021, stood on a hex [60.5] prints. Capacity is NOT carried here: it is rule 36's, by kind (36.12 airfield six, 36.2 strip one, 36.3 basin three, 36.4 alighting area one), and game.air.MAX_CAPACITY is its one home. Consumed by game.oob.charted_air_facilities via game.logistics_data.

air_facilities_60_5._ownership: [60.5] states ownership as GEOGRAPHY and nothing else: 'Players will note that Air facilities may be used by anyone who controls them. All facilities in Egypt belong to the Commonwealth; all those in Libya belong to the Italians. at the start of the game.' So each row carries the COUNTRY it is in and the side that follows from it. The Egypt/Libya frontier is in no data file this project holds, so it was resolved this way and the reasoning is recorded rather than assumed: (a) THE CHART ITSELF PRINTS THE ANCHOR -- it lists Sollum C4021 and Ft. Capuzzo C4020 as two separate Air Landing Strips, and those are the twin frontier posts either side of the wire, Sollum in Egypt and Capuzzo in Libya; (b) THE VICTORY CONDITIONS IN THE NEXT COLUMN OF THE SAME PAGE SETTLE THE BORDERLINE ROWS OUTRIGHT, and they are the strongest evidence here (read on the scan, PDF p.79 third column, 2026-07-22) -- [60.81] requires the Commonwealth to 'take and hold Bardia, Fort Maddalena, Sidi Omar, Sollum and Siwa' for his Decisive Victory, and requires the Italian to 'retain possession of Sollum (C4021) and Fort Maddalena and Giarabub' for his Tactical, so Bardia C4321, Ft. Maddalena C3019, Sidi Omar C3618 and Giarabub C1014 are all AXIS at the start of the game, exactly as transcribed below. (The same sentence puts SOLLUM C4021 in Italian possession too, and this file seeds it ALLIED off [60.5]'s geography rule -- Sollum is the Egyptian post of the twin frontier posts. The two are not in conflict: [60.5] states who the facilities BELONG to at the start, [60.81] describes who is standing on the hex on 15 September 1940, by which date the Italian offensive had already taken Sollum. 36.15 makes the facility non-denominational and game.air.holder reads the CURRENT controller of the hex, so the seeded side is overridden by control the moment control differs. What is NOT checked here is whether the campaign's own deployment gives the Italians C4021 on Game-Turn 1 as [60.81] implies it should -- that is an OOB question, not an air-map one.); (c) the meridian argument, which agrees with (b) and extends it to the two rows the victory conditions do not name: the real frontier is a meridian (25 degrees E), so it is a near-vertical line on this map, and every borderline facility -- Sidi Azeiz C4218, Menastir C4419, Sidi Omar C3618, Ft. Maddalena C3019 and the blank strip C4119 -- lies WEST of Ft. Capuzzo in board pixels (game.coords.to_pixel), hence in Libya; (d) the BOOK corroborates both sides of the line a third time in the neighbouring charts: [60.34] gives the Axis its start-line dumps at Tobruk C4807, Bardia C4321, Derna B5925 and Benghazi, and [60.44] gives the Commonwealth his at Mersa Matruh D3714 and Sidi Barrani C4131. AirFacility.side is only the SEEDED owner in any case: 36.15 makes a facility non-denominational and game.air.holder derives the current holder from control of the hex.
air_facilities_60_5._off_map: SIX ROWS ARE PRINTED 'Off-Map' AND ARE NOT SEEDED (on_map: false), plus the four Tripoli/Tunisia boxes: Abu Seier, Deversoir, Fayid, Ismailia, Kabrit and the Port Said basin. Where the chart prints a hex in parentheses -- E(3433), E(1833), E4033 -- that is the off-map box's entry hex, not the facility's location, and all three of those hexes exist on map E; seeding a facility onto one would put an off-map installation on the playable map, which is the opposite of what the chart says. They are all Commonwealth rear fields (the Delta and the Canal Zone) except the Tripoli/Tunisia boxes, which are the Axis's. The engine has no off-map air box to stand any of them in; that is the same gap data/victory_cities.json records for Tripoli as a supply source. THE RULE BEING DEFERRED IS [36.5] OFF-MAP AIR FACILITIES (docs/rules/36-air-facilities.md lines 46-56; 36.12 cross-references it and 36.5 names Deversoir by name), and it is deferred whole, not merely unmodelled: 36.5 says off-map facilities 'are treated the same as other, on-map facilities' with three exceptions -- (a) 'there are unlimited supplies for airplane maintenance and repair'; (b) they may exceed the SGSU limit up to the limit listed on the field; (c) they may never be destroyed by bombing, though they may be reduced to zero and are rebuilt off the Malta Air Facility Construction Table. [61.44] states the Axis half of (a) in passing -- 'remember, Tripoli/Tunisia air facilities have unlimited Fuel and Ammo'. 36.5(a) MATTERS TO THE HEADLINE: Phase 5's finding is that nothing refills an air dump, and one of the things the book gives and we do not is an unlimited air-maintenance source at five Commonwealth rear fields and Port Said. It sits with 36.3 (trucks to a flying boat basin), 35.15 (first-line trucks attached to an SGSU) and the [60.33]/[60.43] air-facility lorry rows on the unbuilt-faucet list, differing only in that it needs an off-map air box before it needs a rule.
air_facilities_60_5._counts: ROW FOR ROW WITH THE PAGE: 24 Airfield records for the chart's 20 printed table rows plus its closing line 'The four Tripoli/Tunisia boxes (off-map)', 31 Air Landing Strips, 3 Flying Boat Basins, 1 Flying Boat Alighting Area = 59. ON THE PLAYABLE MAP that is 15 Airfields, 31 Air Landing Strips, 2 Flying Boat Basins and 1 Alighting Area = 49 facilities. One record per printed row is the invariant here: the Alexandria row names two hexes and is still ONE record (see its _note), so this file never carries more installations than the chart prints. (The commit that first landed this chart said 'twenty airfields' in its subject line -- that is the count of PRINTED AIRFIELD ROWS, 20, of which 15 are placeable on maps A-E; the four Tripoli/Tunisia boxes and the five Delta/Canal fields are off-map.)

facilities.name: Alexandria
facilities._note: ⚠ OWNER RULING NEEDED -- ONE PRINTED ROW, AND THE ENGINE CANNOT HOLD WHAT IT SAYS. The chart prints a single Airfields row, 'Alexandria  Both hexes, (E3613, 3714)' (scan PDF p.79, re-read 2026-07-22), and E3613/E3714 are the two hexes the city occupies -- the same pair data/victory_cities.json 'auto_win' names. 'ONE airfield occupying both hexes of the city' and 'TWO airfields, one per hex' are both readings of that line and the book does not choose. Our facility model is per-hex (36.15 is captured by occupying ITS hex, 36.14 bombs ITS levels), so it cannot express the first reading and must pick. THE ROW IS SEEDED ONCE, AT THE FIRST PRINTED HEX, because that is the reading that invents nothing: materialising it twice would create twelve 36.12 squadron slots where the chart prints one row, two separately-bombable installations, and one extra share of the [60.44] Commonwealth air pool -- capacity the chart does not print, inferred from the shape of our engine rather than from the page (rule 1: transcribe, never invent). The second hex is recorded in also_printed_hex and is deliberately UNSEEDED: nothing reads it, so if the ruling comes back 'both hexes', that field is where the fix starts. Consequence of the deferral, stated: an Axis capture of E3613 alone would take the whole Alexandria airfield, and no squadron may base on E3714. Both are inert today -- the Delta is 900 km behind the front, no charted SGSU reaches map E (game.oob.seed_sgsus), and no Axis aeroplane in this campaign has the range.

facilities.name: Deversoir
facilities._note: The chart prints the SAME off-map entry hex, E(1833), for Deversoir and for Kabrit. Both are Suez Canal Zone fields on the Great Bitter Lake, so one shared off-map box is a reading the chart supports; neither is seeded either way (off-map).
The chart's City/Village column is a printed em-dash: an airfield at a hex with no named settlement, not a missing name.

facilities.name: Tripoli/Tunisia box 1
facilities._note: The chart closes the Airfields table with 'The four Tripoli/Tunisia boxes (off-map).' They are recorded as four off-map airfields so the chart's set is complete; the engine has no off-map air box to stand them in (the same gap data/victory_cities.json records for the Tripoli supply source), so none is seeded.

facilities.name: El Agheila
facilities._note: TERRAIN FLAG (the map extraction, not the book): data/terrain_A.json reads A1816 as sea. It is the known coastal colour-sampling defect already recorded at game.cna_map:42 and game.campaign_victory:100 -- data/wells.json independently places the VILLAGE of El Agheila on this same hex, and 61.43C calls it a road hex. The chart's hex is kept; the terrain file is the suspect.

facilities.name: Gazala
facilities._note: TERRAIN FLAG: terrain_B.json reads B4933 as sea -- the same coastal sampling defect as El Agheila, and data/wells.json places the village of Gazala on this same hex.

facilities.name: Matten Baggush
facilities._note: TERRAIN FLAG: terrain_D.json reads D3520 as sea -- the same coastal sampling defect, and data/wells.json places the village of Matten Baggush on this same hex.

facilities.name: Bomba
facilities._note: terrain_B.json reads B5331 as sea, and for a FLYING BOAT BASIN that is the right answer: Bomba is the gulf the boats alighted on. Recorded because the same reading is a flag on a LANDING STRIP three rows above -- and because of one consequence: game.scenario's standing convention is that a hex a piece stands on is land ('coastal ports colour-sample as sea'), so the basin's own 36.17 dump turns B5331 CLEAR in the built campaign. It opens no route -- the hex is a one-hex coastal pocket whose only two land neighbours, B5330 and B5430, are already adjacent to each other -- and the three flagged strips were land under that same convention already, each carrying a data/wells.json village.
